System Instructions: Act as a Senior UI/UX Designer and Frontend Engineer. Create a modern, high-performance portfolio website for Yuvraj Tale. The design should be "futuristic yet professional," .

Tech Stack:

Next.js 14 (App Router)

Tailwind CSS

Framer Motion (for smooth entrance animations)

Lucide React (for icons)

Shadcn UI (optional, for clean components)

Content & Sections (Hardcode this data):

1. Hero Section:

Headline: "Building Intelligent Infrastructure for the Web."

Sub-headline: "Hi, I'm Yuvraj Tale. A Final Year CSE Student & Full-Stack Developer specializing in React, Next.js, and Cloud-Native AI Solutions."

Badges/Pills: Display "IBM Gold Medalist" and "Top-10 Hackathon Finalist" prominently near the top.

CTA Buttons: "View Projects" (Primary, Neon Orange) and "Download Resume" (Secondary, Outline).

2. Tech Stack Marquee:

Create a scrolling marquee or clean grid for skills: React, Next.js, TypeScript, Node.js, Supabase, Docker, AWS, GCP, Azure, Redux, Strapi.

3. Featured Project (The "Hero" Project - LocalLens):

Design this section exactly like the provided screenshot style: Dark cards with neon icons.

Title: LocalLens - Intelligent Digital Platform for Tourism

Tagline: "We don't just book tickets. We architect experiences."

Stack: React, Supabase, Google Gemini AI, TypeScript.

Features Grid (Use these exact titles):

Neural Manifests: Gemini-powered hyper-personalized itineraries.

The Human Layer: Verified guide interface with Supabase Auth.

Visual Intelligence: Multimodal AI allowing users to scan monuments for history.

Discovery Engine: Cultural heatmaps moving beyond "Top 10" lists.

Link: Include a "Live Demo" button.

4. Other Notable Projects (Bento Grid Layout):

Edu-Verse (LMS): Low-bandwidth e-learning platform using Next.js & Redux. Highlight: "Reduced server dependency by integrating YouTube backend."

Future-flow (Algo-Trade): Trading dashboard with Python backend & AngelOne API.

Gorgeous Beauty Parlour: Mobile-first local business site (83% performance boost).

5. Experience Timeline:

Pitchkaro (React Developer Intern): Dec 2024 – Mar 2025. Built scalable components & CI/CD pipelines.

Datagami (Frontend Developer Intern): May 2024 – July 2024. Visualized GitHub collaboration data & built host websites using Strapi/Next.js.

6. Certifications (Grid of Cards):

Google Cloud Computing Foundations

AWS Storage & Data Management

NPTEL Software Engineering

NPTEL Business Intelligence

(Add a subtle glowing border to these cards).

7. Contact/Footer:

Simple form or "Let's Connect" section.

Links: GitHub (Yuvraj070), LinkedIn, Email (yuvrajtale03@gmail.com).

Visual Style:

Background: Deep space blue/black gradient.

Typography: Sans-serif, clean (Inter or Geist font).

Animations: Elements should fade in upwards as the user scrolls. The "LocalLens" cards should have a subtle hover glow effect.

**Defining the Project Scope**

Okay, I've got a solid grasp of the initial request. My focus right now is on nailing down the specific scope: a high-performance, futuristic portfolio website for Yuvraj Tale. Understanding the desired role and the stylistic direction are the cornerstones for the next steps. I'm moving toward mapping the user flows and core functionalities.


**Refining the Tech Stack**

I'm solidifying the technical implementation. I'm choosing a deep space blue/black gradient background with neon orange accents and clean sans-serif typography. I've chosen React 18+ with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, and Lucide React. I've adapted to the prompt's SPA requirement, and I am ensuring that the style of Next.js modularity is retained. I am planning to build with hardcoded data extracted from the prompt and the provided resume text. The site will have a Hero section, Tech Stack Marquee, Featured Project, Other Projects, Experience Timeline, Certifications and Contact/Footer.

I am developing the basic structure, including metadata.json, index.html, index.tsx, and App.tsx, which will compose the key sections.


**Structuring the Components**

I'm structuring the reusable UI components now, and planning the core pages. I've broken down each section into its own `.tsx` file, including the Hero, TechStack, FeaturedProject, ProjectGrid, Experience, Certifications, and Contact components. The goal is to build base UI elements (like Button, Card, and Badge) with styling in `ui/`. I'm simulating a Shadcn UI setup for consistent design.

\n\n

**Implementing Design & Styling**

I am implementing the design with Tailwind. I have identified the background as slate-950, or a radial gradient. For primary color, I will use neon orange. The text will use a mix of slate-200 and white. I'm using default Tailwind system sans-serif fonts, and implementing framer-motion for scroll reveals. I'm working to make the site feel smooth.


**Outlining Key Elements**

I'm now outlining UI elements. The Hero will have a large headline and pill-shaped badges. I'm imagining a marquee for the Tech Stack. Featured Project should be a dark card with neon-icon grid items. Experience calls for a vertical timeline with dots. The Certification cards need subtle border glows. I've been refining content based on OCR, especially for project details and contact info, and I'm adding a Gemini-powered blurb to LocalLens.


**Constructing Page Sections**

I'm now implementing the site's structural sections. I've integrated Inter from Google Fonts for a clean look. In `App.tsx`, I've set up the gradient background. I'm focusing on the `components` folder, starting with the Hero section and adding fade-up effects with Framer Motion and relevant badges. Next, I am building the TechStack, using flex-wrap with a hover pulse animation. The FeaturedProject is underway with its grid items and Lucide icons. I'll build the ProjectGrid next with a Bento layout.


**Finalizing Core Features**

I'm now implementing the Experience timeline with a vertical border and dot markers. Next, I'll be constructing the Certifications grid with glowing borders as requested, and a simple contact form with social links. I'm focusing on ensuring all requested constraints are met. I'm checking that the single XML block and React 18 roots are implemented. I'm ensuring TypeScript, neon orange theming, and specific content are incorporated.
